item.title: The Case Of April Kauffman
item.description:
In 2012, New Jersey radio host April Kauffman was found shot to death inside her home in Linwood. Investigators initially struggled to identify a motive, and the case remained unsolved for years as suspicion surrounded multiple people connected to her life.
Authorities later alleged her husband, Dr. James Kauffman, was tied to a criminal network involving illegal drug operations and had arranged the killing. Prosecutors said financial issues and personal conflicts played a role in the murder plot.
Several men were eventually charged in connection with the case. Dr. Kauffman died by suicide in jail before standing trial, leaving many questions surrounding April’s death and the broader investigation.

media:content.media:title: The Case Of April Kauffman
media:content.media:description:
In 2012, New Jersey radio host April Kauffman was found shot to death inside her home in Linwood. Investigators initially struggled to identify a motive, and the case remained unsolved for years as suspicion surrounded multiple people connected to her life.
Authorities later alleged her husband, Dr. James Kauffman, was tied to a criminal network involving illegal drug operations and had arranged the killing. Prosecutors said financial issues and personal conflicts played a role in the murder plot.
Several men were eventually charged in connection with the case. Dr. Kauffman died by suicide in jail before standing trial, leaving many questions surrounding April’s death and the broader investigation.

item.title: Andrea Sneiderman Was Convicted of Lying About Her Husband's Murder
item.description: Rusty Sneiderman was shot and killed outside a Dunwoody, Georgia preschool in November 2010. His wife Andrea Sneiderman's former boss Hemy Neuman was convicted of the murder. Murder charges against Andrea were dropped in 2013, but she was convicted of perjury and hindering the investigation and sentenced to five years in prison. She was released on parole in June 2014 after serving 10 months.

media:content.media:title: Andrea Sneiderman Was Convicted of Lying About Her Husband's Murder
media:content.media:description: Rusty Sneiderman was shot and killed outside a Dunwoody, Georgia preschool in November 2010. His wife Andrea Sneiderman's former boss Hemy Neuman was convicted of the murder. Murder charges against Andrea were dropped in 2013, but she was convicted of perjury and hindering the investigation and sentenced to five years in prison. She was released on parole in June 2014 after serving 10 months.

item.title: Found After 40 Years! Holly Clouse Case
item.description: In 2022, authorities identified Holly Marie Clouse, who had been missing since 1980. Her parents' remains were found in Texas in 1981, but their identities were unknown until DNA testing. Holly was located safe and reunited with her biological family, while the investigation into her parents' deaths remains open.

media:content.media:title: Found After 40 Years! Holly Clouse Case
media:content.media:description: In 2022, authorities identified Holly Marie Clouse, who had been missing since 1980. Her parents' remains were found in Texas in 1981, but their identities were unknown until DNA testing. Holly was located safe and reunited with her biological family, while the investigation into her parents' deaths remains open.

item.title: What Really Happened to Alonzo Brooks?
item.description: In 2004, 23-year-old Alonzo Brooks disappeared after attending a party in La Cygne, Kansas. His remains were located by family members in a nearby creek a month later. After the case was reopened in 2020, the FBI reclassified the death as a homicide, though no charges have been filed and the investigation remains active.

media:content.media:title: What Really Happened to Alonzo Brooks?
media:content.media:description: In 2004, 23-year-old Alonzo Brooks disappeared after attending a party in La Cygne, Kansas. His remains were located by family members in a nearby creek a month later. After the case was reopened in 2020, the FBI reclassified the death as a homicide, though no charges have been filed and the investigation remains active.

item.title: What Happened To Oakley Carlson?
item.description:
In 2021, 5 year old Oakley Carlson disappeared from her home in Washington, but authorities say she was not reported missing for nearly 10 months. Concerns grew after a school principal noticed no one had seen her in months and requested a welfare check.
Investigators said her parents gave conflicting statements about her whereabouts, and both remain persons of interest. Despite extensive searches, Oakley has never been found, and in 2025 she was legally declared dead while the investigation remained active.

media:content.media:title: What Happened To Oakley Carlson?
media:content.media:description:
In 2021, 5 year old Oakley Carlson disappeared from her home in Washington, but authorities say she was not reported missing for nearly 10 months. Concerns grew after a school principal noticed no one had seen her in months and requested a welfare check.
Investigators said her parents gave conflicting statements about her whereabouts, and both remain persons of interest. Despite extensive searches, Oakley has never been found, and in 2025 she was legally declared dead while the investigation remained active.

item.title: Brittani Marcell: She Helped Find The Man Who Did It!
item.description:
In 2008, 17 year old Brittani Marcell was attacked outside her New Mexico home and suffered severe head injuries that affected her memory. Investigators struggled for years to identify who was responsible as the case remained unsolved.
Despite lasting trauma, Brittani later began recovering memories connected to the attack. Her recollections helped investigators identify a suspect, leading to the arrest of a former family friend more than a decade later.
In 2022, the suspect was convicted and sentenced to prison. The case drew attention due to Brittani’s role in helping solve the investigation years after surviving the attack.

media:content.media:title: Brittani Marcell: She Helped Find The Man Who Did It!
media:content.media:description:
In 2008, 17 year old Brittani Marcell was attacked outside her New Mexico home and suffered severe head injuries that affected her memory. Investigators struggled for years to identify who was responsible as the case remained unsolved.
Despite lasting trauma, Brittani later began recovering memories connected to the attack. Her recollections helped investigators identify a suspect, leading to the arrest of a former family friend more than a decade later.
In 2022, the suspect was convicted and sentenced to prison. The case drew attention due to Brittani’s role in helping solve the investigation years after surviving the attack.

item.title: The Case of Jennifer and Adrianna Wix
item.description:
In March 2004, Jennifer Wix and her 2 year old daughter Adrianna disappeared from Robertson County, Tennessee after leaving the home where they had been staying. Family members said Jennifer planned to travel to another residence but never arrived.
Investigators later focused on people connected to Jennifer’s personal life, though no one has been charged. Over the years, searches and interviews continued as authorities worked to determine what happened.
Despite ongoing investigations and public appeals, neither Jennifer nor Adrianna has ever been found. The case remains open, with law enforcement continuing to seek information about their disappearance.

media:content.media:title: The Case of Jennifer and Adrianna Wix
media:content.media:description:
In March 2004, Jennifer Wix and her 2 year old daughter Adrianna disappeared from Robertson County, Tennessee after leaving the home where they had been staying. Family members said Jennifer planned to travel to another residence but never arrived.
Investigators later focused on people connected to Jennifer’s personal life, though no one has been charged. Over the years, searches and interviews continued as authorities worked to determine what happened.
Despite ongoing investigations and public appeals, neither Jennifer nor Adrianna has ever been found. The case remains open, with law enforcement continuing to seek information about their disappearance.

item.title: Emmett Corrigan: A Case Of Love, Betrayal And Tragedy
item.description:
In 2011, Idaho attorney Emmett Corrigan was shot and killed outside a Walgreens after his affair with married paralegal Kandi Hall was discovered. Prosecutors said her husband, Robert Hall, confronted the pair before the deadly shooting.
The case drew national attention due to the affair, courtroom testimony, and the families involved. Robert Hall was later convicted of second degree murder and sentenced to 30 years in prison.

media:content.media:title: Emmett Corrigan: A Case Of Love, Betrayal And Tragedy
media:content.media:description:
In 2011, Idaho attorney Emmett Corrigan was shot and killed outside a Walgreens after his affair with married paralegal Kandi Hall was discovered. Prosecutors said her husband, Robert Hall, confronted the pair before the deadly shooting.
The case drew national attention due to the affair, courtroom testimony, and the families involved. Robert Hall was later convicted of second degree murder and sentenced to 30 years in prison.
